text: does this mean anything? i have always thought that my fantasies of being a women have just been a fetish, but lately when i finish cross dressing i still have feelings towards it. usually i only wanted to be a women when i am horny, but that feeling hasn't been going away. does this mean anything?

so im trans (amab) and i thought i was bisexual but since ive started to feel feminine my attraction to men have dropped and i only seem to like women nowadays (although my attraction to women has always been far greater than men except for the time i thought i was gay) . im starting to think that im gay but the thought of being gay frightens me because i want to be seen as bisexual. i can only be romantically attracted to boys if i can see myself as a boy and i only have minimal sexual attraction towards them, even then i have very specific tastes and standards for them in order for me to be attracted and not feel dysphoric. i took the kinsey scale test  because i wanted to know how i scored and i got a 5 (however i know the kinsey scale is *severely* outdated), when i identified as male my score was 3. maybe i˜m more attracted to women now because i want to have everything that cis women have (if that makes more sense), their bodies, their personality, their everything, it doesnt help that when i identified as a bisexual cis boy i really wanted to be in a lesbian relationship but i was disappointed that i couldnt because of me being male (which now i interpret as a trans foreshadowing). maybe im gay + biromantic or something?? the realisation that i might be gay is quite scary and i might need your help.  **tl;dr:** since ive started identifying as female my attraction to men has significantly dropped and im worried i might be gay.

i hate my life i hate being a boy so much. looking at my genitalia makes me so uncomfortable. my body hair makes me so upset. i have this cute little feminine body and everyone says i’m beautiful and not handsome and i love it but then i look down and see it and then i see the hair and it makes me so upset.

looking for discussions about dysphoria little background info: afab, 24, nonbinary (graygender / lacking gender; she/they pronouns), plus-size, was raised mormon for \~16 years, virgin (hope this is the right subreddit, posted on lgbt last night; feel free to rec me more subreddits as well as i am finally looking to explore this side of myself) i have never been a tomboy. i was quite feminine as a young girl, and because of the brainwashing of the church i wanted to be a wife, mother, nurturer etc. however as i grew into my own, my brother educating me about the real world (i was always soooo naive/oblivious), and especially taking sociology college courses i realized who i am / want to be: an intersectional feminist, every body is beautiful....except for my own. i'm pansexual and only a couple years ago i realized i'm not cis. i **latched onto** the term graygender. as i have grown into who i am today, i have realized multiple things: i don't want kids, at least not from my own body and def not anytime soon ; i don't want to be *perceived* as a female because i am not inherently feminine ; and therefore i don't want to be judged based on western feminine beauty standards. now i am totally cis-passing, f-cup bra (no binder or even a desire to wear one, i'd like to breathe tyvm), curves, shoulder-length or longer hair, some feminine clothes while some neutral/male v-necks, no makeup. however i am not interested in transitioning, as i said i am graygender which just means i don't want... either. i don't want a penis but i am not happy with my vagina either??? has anyone else experienced this before? i don't want a dick but my vagina is awful!!! periods can kiss my ass!! i don't think i'm agender because i don't feel so strongly that i *need* to be they/them 24/7, like i'm not telling my return-missionary mormon cousins or my grandparents to call me gender-neutral pronouns, just those close to me, but i don't want to be embarrassed or shy *introducing* myself as they/them. does that make sense? because of being raised religious, even though it was a decade ago, i still feel shame for my sexuality, for wanting to feel good, for wanting to explore my body. i realized yesterday that not only do i hate my vagina, i think i'm scared of it. through masturbation i have learned to 'deal with' my breasts, they make me feel good whatever, but i've never orgasmed and quite honestly want to avoid my vagina. this hit me hard because lately i have been thinking of becoming sexually active, and i finally need to start thinking about these things, sharing my body with another, and it terrifies me!! for context, i didn't start masturbating *or* using tampons until i was \~18 (two whole years after i left the church!). is this internalized misogyny? is it just my low self esteem, 'you don't deserve happiness/feeling good' thoughts? **can anyone relate?** i'm not trans, i don't want to transition, i'm not ftm, i don't have penis envy. and here i go quoting a tiktok: 'what are my pronouns? none of your business. don't talk to me, don't talk about me, i am a secret." i guess i am looking for like-minded individuals; do not have to be graygender, afab, a specific orientation etc. it may be easier to connect with people with vaginas, but i'm just looking for discussions really. a kind ear. as graygender is more ambivalence, i don't usually *search* for gender resources, but it just hit really fucking hard and i would just like second opinions or people to share their own stories!!! please, tell me about your life, your success stories, relationships with others or with your own bodies. i'd love to hear, really. feel free to pm or im me 3 **tl;dr graygender bitch looking to talk with others who have experienced dysphoria, about accepting their bodies and overcoming shame, fear, embarrassment, especially about genitalia or from religious backgrounds**
